sglang.title: Fix scale_step_k computation in the fp8_kernel
sglang.author: Muqi1029
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
According to the kernel design, group_k is expected to be divisible by BLOCK_SIZE_K. However, when BLOCK_SIZE_K is smaller than group_k, scale_step_k is always computed as 0, which prevents the scaling pointer from advancing.
For example, with BLOCK_SIZE_K = 64 and group_k = 128, the curr

sglang.title: [JIT Kernel][Feature] Support JIT custom all reduce (rewrite as v2)
sglang.author: DarkSharpness

Motivation

Modifications

This PR implements a clean version of custom all reduce which is highly configurable (we can set the number SMs, the recommended CTA size). We also integrate post-hopper features like PDL into the custom-all-reduce, which improves the latency by up to 40% in small batch si

sglang.title: Add compile-time 256-bit vector guard for pre-Blackwell
sglang.author: xingsy97
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Follow-up from #19770 review discussion. On pre-Blackwell GPUs (SM < 100), CUDA only supports 128-bit vector load/store. The 32-byte AlignedVector path compiles but should never run. This PR adds a compile-time guard so invalid 256-bit instantiations are caught at compile time rather than

vllm.title: [ROCm] Enable wvSplitK skinny GEMM kernel for RDNA4/gfx1x decode
vllm.author: laudney
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Enable the wvSplitK and wvSplitKQ skinny GEMM kernels on RDNA (gfx11/gfx12) hardware for decode-phase GEMMs (M=1..4). Previously these kernels were gfx9-only (MI-series), with RDNA falling through to torch.nn.functional.linear (unquantized) or generic torch._scaled_mm (FP8).
~15% decode toke

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Reject channelwise quantization (group_size <= 0) in ExllamaLinearKernel
vllm.author: mgehre-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
ExllamaLinearKernel.can_implement did not reject channelwise quantization where group_size=-1. Python's modulo semantics mean N % -1 == 0 for any N, so the existing divisibility check silently passed. At runtime, process_weights_after_loading then computed groups = K // -1 = -K and crashed with:
Run

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix ConchLinearKernel channelwise quantization (group_size=-1)
vllm.author: mgehre-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt: ConchLinearKernel.apply_weights passed group_size=-1 directly to the conch mixed_precision_gemm kernel for channelwise quantization. Inside the Triton kernel this computes stride_mul = block_k / group_size = 64 / -1 = -64, causing all K-blocks after the first to read scale values from out-of-bounds

tests/models/multimodal/generation/test_common.py passes for Isaac 0.2. (Isaac 0.1 is failing on main because of incorrect safetensors index).
Model initialization and processor tests should pass for Kimi-K2.5.
Test Result


 Essent

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Disable monolithic TRTLLM MoE for Renormalize routing (#37591)
vllm.author: vadiklyutiy
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The FlashInfer TRTLLM monolithic MoE kernel (trtllm_fp8_block_scale_moe) produces incorrect expert routing when all router logits are negative. This causes 0% GSM8K accuracy for Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8 and Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-FP8 with DP+EP.
This PR removes Renormalize and RenormalizeNaive from Tr

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Setting some mi325_4 tests back to optional (in parity with upstream)
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Setting some tests optional so that we de-congest the 4 MI325 agent pool. These TGs are already optional on upstream vLLM CI
cc @kenroche

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2] Support Streaming Inputs
vllm.author: santiramos27
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
MRv2 doesn't handle the case where a streaming input update arrives for a request that already exists. When the scheduler sends a streaming update (same req_id via scheduled_new_reqs), add_requests() blindly allocates a new slot without freeing the old one, leaking a free_indices entry each

Remove unused dead code, all in private function

vllm serve models/GLM-5-NVFP4   -tp 8   --trust-remote-code   --enable-auto-tool-choice   --tool-call-parser glm47   --reasoning-parser glm45

vllm.title: elastic_ep: Fix issues with repeated scale up/down cycles
vllm.author: itayalroy
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes two issues found when testing repeated scale up/down cycles:


PyNCCL communicators were never explicitly destroyed during
elastic EP reconfigurations. Over repeated scale up/down cycles,
this causes CUDA OOM crashes. Fixed by cleaning up PyNCCL correctly.


Elastic EP EPLB suppression

vllm.title: [Responses API] Add kv_transfer_params for PD disaggregation
vllm.author: bongwoobak
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add kv_transfer_params to the Responses API (/v1/responses) for PD disaggregation support. The Chat Completions API already supports this, but the Responses API does not, preventing PD disaggregation through it.
Follows the same pattern as ChatCompletionRequest/ChatCompletionResponse:

Respo
